stories.title: Peace talks are in doubt as the U.S. seizes an Iranian ship
stories.description: President Trump said a U.S. delegation will head to Pakistan to resume talks to end the war with Iran, but Tehran expressed reluctance after the U.S. seized one of its cargo ships in the Strait of Hormuz.

stories.title: U.S. seizes Iranian cargo ship. And, tariff refund portal launches
stories.description: U.S. forces seized an Iranian-flagged cargo vessel in the Strait of Hormuz. And, an online government portal for processing tariff refunds launches today.

stories.title: Ukraine's fast-growing defense tech sector takes center stage in Iran war
stories.description: While Ukraine seeks to shift its image from not only a recipient of military aid but also a provider, its battle-tested, anti-drone tech is taking center stage in the Iran war.

stories.title: U.K police investigating if attacks in London are the work of Iranian proxies
stories.description: U.K. police said Sunday they are investigating whether a string of arson attacks on Jewish sites in London are the work of Iranian proxies, the latest of which caused minor damage on Saturday night.

stories.title: U.S. says it seized an Iranian cargo ship, ceasefire expires soon
stories.description: The two-week ceasefire between the US and Iran is set to expire Wednesday. And the two sides seem far from a resolution. President Trump says the US has taken custody of an Iranian vessel.

stories.title: U.S. seizes Iranian cargo ship in Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: The U.S. has taken custody of an Iranian-flagged cargo ship in the Strait of Hormuz after firing on its engine room, President Trump said in a post on social media.

stories.title: Tehran will never cede control of Strait of Hormuz, senior Iranian politician tells BBC
stories.description: Lyse Doucet speaks to Ebrahim Azizi, who says Iran "will decide the right of passage" through the crucial shipping route.

stories.title: Outrage over Israeli soldier's vandalism of Jesus statue in Lebanon
stories.description: The Israeli military says it views with "great severity" the actions of the soldier, as the US ambassador demands "swift" consequences.

stories.title: French peacekeeper killed in southern Lebanon
stories.description: President Macron blames the attack on Hezbollah. The Iran-backed armed group denies "any connection" to the incident.

stories.title: A moment of risk and opportunity: How two ceasefires could boost US-Iran talks
stories.description: Some kind of diplomatic progress is now under way, but is it enough to prevent a return to war?

stories.title: Ceasefire with Israel brings respite to Lebanon, but obstacles to peace remain
stories.description: The halt to weeks of fighting is being celebrated in Lebanon, but it will be difficult to ensure it lasts.

stories.title: What we know about the Israel-Lebanon ceasefire
stories.description: A 10-day truce between the two countries is now in effect, with Iran-backed Hezbollah voicing support, as negotiations continue between the US and Iran.

stories.title: Lyse Doucet: Under fragile ceasefire, Iranians wonder if US deal can be done
stories.description: The BBC's chief international correspondent reports from Iran as diplomatic efforts to avoid a return to war intensify.

stories.title: UK and France to lead defensive mission in Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: Sir Keir Starmer has said further details of the plan will come after a meeting in London next week.

stories.title: Israeli triple-tap strike kills three paramedics in Lebanon, officials say
stories.description: Lebanon's government condemns as a "flagrant crime" the killing of the paramedics, one of whom featured in a BBC report.

stories.title: Sri Lanka repatriates 238 Iranian sailors stranded after US torpedo attack
stories.description: The survivors of the US attack on Iranian vessel Iris Dena, which claimed the lives of 104 people, were among those sent home.

stories.title: 'Hierarchy of sovereignty': Israel claims authority over 'which Lebanese areas are inhabitable'
stories.description: Carys Garland is pleased to welcome Nadim Houry, Executive Director of the Arab Reform Initiative (ARI). According to Houry, the “Yellow Line doctrine” is not a security measure, but rather a military tool for territorial control. Drawing on recent developments in Gaza, Syria, an...

stories.title: Ceasefire at risk after US seizes Iranian ship, Iran shuns peace talks
stories.description: A ceasefire between the United States and Iran appeared in jeopardy on Monday after the US said it had seized an Iranian cargo ship that tried to run its blockade and Tehran vowed to retaliate, refusing for now to join new peace talks. Iranian Foreign Ministry spokesman Esmaeil B...

stories.title: Israel army warns Lebanese against returning to south
stories.description: Israel's military on Monday warned Lebanese civilians against returning to dozens of villages in southern Lebanon, claiming Hezbollah's activities in the area were violating a ceasefire agreement struck last week. Thousands of displaced residents have begun making their way back ...

stories.title: Iran-US ceasefire talks: Nuclear program remains sticking point
stories.description: Differences over ​Iran's nuclear programme ​still remain, a senior Iranian source told Reuters ​on ‌Monday, ⁠as Iran and ‌the United States try ⁠to reach a lasting peace with ​the two-week ‌ceasefire ending soon. The senior Iranian source ‌said that Tehran's "defensive capabiliti...

stories.title: Israel army says established 'Yellow Line' in Lebanon, as in Gaza
stories.description: Israel's military said Saturday it had established a "Yellow Line" demarcation in southern Lebanon, similar to one separating its forces from Hamas-held areas in Gaza, adding it had hit a "terrorist cell" operating near its troops along the line. Israel and Lebanon agreed to a 10...

stories.title: US seizes Iranian-flagged cargo ship
stories.description: The #US said it had seized an Iranian-flagged cargo ship that tried to get past its naval blockade near the Strait of #Hormuz on Sunday. #Iran vowed to retaliate, throwing planned peace talks between the two countries into question.

stories.title: 'Situation is dire' for displaced people in Lebanon, journalist says
stories.description: Less than a week after a fragile ceasefire was announced in Lebanon, a senior Anglophone journalist in the country has spoken to FRANCE 24 about the devastation people are seeing as they start to return home. Thousands of homes have been destroyed in the conflict, and more than a...

stories.title: 'US military leverage a risky tactic': Bombs, military escalation will not bring Iran 'to the table'
stories.description: Genie Godula is pleased to welcome Jim Townsend, Adjunct Senior Fellow in the CNAS Transatlantic Security Program and former Deputy Assistant Secretary of Defense (DASD) for European and NATO Policy. According to Townsend, US–Iran negotiations are shaped by tactical manoeuvering:...

stories.title: Iran expands limited internet access but restrictions remain for most
stories.description: More Iranians being sold 'privilege' of limited internet access, but most remain in the dark.

stories.title: Lebanon Latest: Israel establishes ‘yellow line’ in south
stories.description: Al Jazeera’s Zeina Khod brings you the latest on the fragile ceasefire between Israel and Hezbollah.

stories.title: Iranian FM spokesman says ceasefire ‘violated’ by US in shipment seizure
stories.description: Iran is accusing the US of ‘ceasefire violations’, with Ministry of Foreign Affairs spokesman Esmail Baghaei.

stories.title: ‘Israel never talked me into the war with Iran,’ Trump says
stories.description: US president suggests attacking Iran was his own decision as war struggles to garner public support amid energy crisis.

stories.title: Cloud over US-Iran talks: What are the key sticking points?
stories.description: Trump announces new talks with Iran as fragile Pakistan-brokered ceasefire raises stakes for future negotiations.

stories.title: Tight security in Islamabad as US-Iran talks set to go ahead
stories.description: Security is on lockdown in Islamabad where preparations are being made for new US‑Iran talks.

stories.title: What we know about the US capture of Iranian vessel near Hormuz
stories.description: US releases video showing the capture of Iranian-flagged ship near Hormuz, Trump says it tried to breach its blockade.

stories.title: Iran says it has ‘no plans’ for negotiations with the US
stories.description: Iran says it has ‘no plans’ for negotiations with the US

stories.title: India, South Korea plan $50bn trade push with new deals
stories.description: India and South Korea said on Monday that they would boost their economic ties by expanding cooperation in energy, critical minerals, shipbuilding, semiconductors and steel as they seek to double their trade to $50 billion by 2030. New Delhi and Seoul also agreed to resume and st...

stories.title: Uncertainty prevails over talks amid mixed signals from Iran; Trump says US delegation en route to Islamabad
stories.description: Key developments Trump says JD Vance and an American delegation en route to Islamabad for talks Iran says it has yet to decide whether it will attend next round of talks with US; parliament speaker highlights lack of trust in Washington Tight security in Islamabad for arrival of ...

stories.title: Israel condemns soldier’s desecration of crucifix in south Lebanon
stories.description: Israel’s Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu, the foreign minister and the military on Monday condemned the desecration of a crucifix after it was smashed by an Israeli soldier in a Christian village in southern Lebanon. A photo that emerged online over the weekend shows a soldier ...

stories.title: Assault on vessel overshadows preparation for round two of US-Iran dialogue
stories.description: • Both sides offer differing accounts of incident in sea of Oman• Tehran ‘rejects’ participation over ‘Washington’s excessive demands, unrealistic expectations’• American delegation expected in Islamabad this evening; advance teams already in town• Trump’s remarks sow confusion o...

stories.title: Israel reoccupies evacuated West Bank settlement
stories.description: SA NUR/MADRID: Israeli ministers on Sunday officially reopened Sa-Nur, a settlement in the occupied West Bank that was evacuated 20 years ago, marking the occasion with defiant declarations against Palestinian statehood and calls to resettle Gaza. stories.title: Lebanon restores lifelines as Israel extends demolition
stories.description: • Beirut reopens vital Qasmiyeh bridge, road linking the south after strikes• Israel pushes deeper, mapping buffer zone inside Lebanese territory BEIRUT: Lebanon’s military announced on Sunday it had reopened a key road and bridge destroyed by Israeli strikes in the country’s sou...

stories.title: Iran says no decision yet on joining Pakistan talks with U.S.
stories.description: The two sides are expected to meet in the coming days in Islamabad, for which Pakistani authorities have taken elaborate security measures

stories.title: Iran-Israel war live: Israel never talked me into war with Iran, says Trump
stories.description: Vance, U.S. team en route to Islamabad for Iran talks, says Trump, Iran says 'no decision' yet on joining new round of U.S. peace talks

stories.title: U.K. calls for 'toll-free' Strait of Hormuz
stories.description: U.K. Foreign Secretary Yvette Cooper arrived in Japan for the final leg of what has been branded as an "intensified shuttle diplomacy tour" to advance coordination with key allies on shared geopolitical, economic and energy security priorities

stories.title: Iran not planning to attend talks with U.S. in Pakistan
stories.description: Donald Trump announced that a U.S. destroyer fired at a massive Iranian-flagged cargo ship after it tried to get past the U.S. naval blockade

stories.title: Watch: Islamabad tightens security ahead of possible US-Iran talks
U.S., allied forces kick off combat drills with Philippines despite Washington's focus on Iran
stories.description: The large-scale combat drills between the U.S. and Philippines will expand this year to include other militaries, including from Japan, France and Canada, which have signed visiting forces agreements with Manila
